subsectors.name: Computer Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide consulting or integration services to other businesses relating to information technology. Includes providers of computer-system design, systems integration, network and systems operations, cloud computing, distributed ledger technology (DLT) consulting and integration, data management and storage, repair services and technical support.

subsectors.name: Software
subsectors.definition: Publishers and distributors of computer software for home or corporate use. Excludes computer game producers.

subsectors.name: Consumer Digital Services
subsectors.definition: Companies involved in digital platforms that primarily generate revenue from advertising, content delivery and other virtual products for consumers. To a lesser extent, they generate subscription fee revenue through related services offered by the platform and/or by the advertisers to utilise data content and customer insight.

subsectors.name: Semiconductors
subsectors.definition: Producers and distributors of semiconductors and other integrated chips, including other products related to the semiconductor industry, such as motherboards. Excludes makers of printed circuit boards.

subsectors.name: Electronic Components
subsectors.definition: Companies involved in the application of high-technology parts to finished products, including printed circuit boards. Excludes communications-related equipment and technology parts for consumer electronics.

subsectors.name: Production Technology Equipment
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers of tools and/or devices that are used in the creation of semiconductors, lasers, photonics, wafers, and other high-technology components, including semiconductor capital equipment.

subsectors.name: Computer Hardware
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of computers, servers, mainframes, workstations and other computer hardware and subsystems, such as mass-storage drives, mice, keyboards, printers. Companies in this group can provide diverse applications for consumer and commercial use. Including companies providing computing hardware for cryptocurrency mining.

subsectors.name: Electronic Office Equipment
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of electronic office equipment including photocopiers and fax machines.

subsectors.name: Telecommunications Equipment
subsectors.definition: Makers and distributors of high-technology communication products, including satellites, mobile telephones, fibre optics, switching devices, local and wide-area networks, teleconferencing equipment and connectivity devices for computers, including hubs and routers.

subsectors.name: Cable Television Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that distribute media content through cable and wireless networks, accessed through computers or televisions.

subsectors.name: Telecommunications Services
subsectors.definition: Providers of mobile and fixed-line telephone services, including cellular, satellite and paging services. Includes wireless tower companies that own, operate and lease mobile site towers to multiple wireless service providers. Includes companies that primarily provides telephone services through the internet. Excludes companies whose primary business is internet access.

subsectors.name: Health Care Facilities
subsectors.definition: Owners and operators of primary healthcare property, community hospitals, retirement homes, nursing homes, and related medical businesses.

subsectors.name: Health Care Management Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that offer managed healthcare benefits and services including health management organisations (HMO), including state sponsored programs.

subsectors.name: Health Care Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide various specialised disease management services to physicians, health plans, and hospitals. Primary business lines in this category can also include general consultation services, paramedical services, operation of health portals and distribution of health food products.

subsectors.name: Health Care: Misc.
subsectors.definition: Includes healthcare companies that are not classified in the healthcare facilities, healthcare management services or healthcare services subsectors.

subsectors.name: Medical Equipment
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of medical devices such as MRI scanners, prosthetics, pacemakers, X-ray machines and other non-disposable medical devices.

subsectors.name: Medical Supplies
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of medical supplies used by healthcare providers and the general public. Includes makers of contact lenses, eyeglass lenses, bandages and other disposable medical supplies.

subsectors.name: Medical Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that operate and manage medical labs and testing services.

subsectors.name: Biotechnology
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in research into and development of biological substances for the purposes of drug discovery and diagnostic development, and which derive the majority of their revenue from either the sale or licensing of these drugs and diagnostic tools.

subsectors.name: Pharmaceuticals
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers of prescription or over-the-counter drugs, such as aspirin, cold remedies and birth control pills. Includes vaccine producers but excludes vitamin producers, which are classified under food products subsector.

subsectors.name: Cannabis Producers
subsectors.definition: Companies that engage in cannabis cultivation, cannabis distribution including dispensaries, the processing and distribution of cannabis plants, and the creation of cannabis derivative products. Companies that primarily engage in the research, development and manufacturing of cannabis (THC/CBD) based drugs are classified in the biotechnology or the pharmaceuticals subsector.

subsectors.name: Banks
subsectors.definition: Companies with a banking license which derive the greatest portion of their revenue from providing a broad range of banking services, including retail banking, loans, and money transmissions. Does not include investment banks which are classified under Investment Services.

subsectors.name: Consumer Lending
subsectors.definition: Companies that are not Banks that provide financial services to consumers or businesses including payday loans, student loans, automobile loans, business loans etc.

subsectors.name: Mortgage Finance
subsectors.definition: Companies that are not Banks that provide mortgages, mortgage insurance and other related services.

subsectors.name: Financial Data Providers
subsectors.definition: Companies which provide financial decision support tools for investment institutions. Including financial database operators and index data providers.

subsectors.name: Diversified Financial Services
subsectors.definition: Companies providing a range of financial services, none of which is the dominant business line. See Rule 4.2.3 for full details. Diversified Investment Holding companies engaged in acquiring equity stakes of securities are also classified in this group.

subsectors.name: Asset Managers and Custodians
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide custodial, trustee and other related fiduciary services. i.e. mutual fund and private investment management. Also includes companies engaged in private equity and venture capital.

subsectors.name: Investment Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide trading and brokerage services for financial assets such as equities, commodities, debts, currency, cryptocurrency. Also includes the operators of stock, currency, and other financial market exchanges as well as investment banks.

subsectors.name: Mortgage REITs: Diversified
subsectors.definition: Mortgage REITs that invest in a combination of segments with no specific dominance over one or the other.

subsectors.name: Mortgage REITs: Commercial
subsectors.definition: REITs primarily involved in lending money to commercial real estate owners and operators directly or indirectly through the purchase of mortgages, mortgage backed securities and other mortgage related assets.

subsectors.name: Mortgage REITs: Residential
subsectors.definition: REITs primarily involved in lending money to residential real estate owners and operators directly or indirectly through the purchase of mortgages, mortgage backed securities and other mortgage related assets.

subsectors.name: Closed End Investments
subsectors.definition: Corporate closed-ended investment entities identified under distinguishing legislation, such as investment trusts and venture capital trusts.

sectors.name: Open End and Miscellaneous Investment Vehicles

subsectors.name: Open End and Miscellaneous Investment Vehicles
subsectors.definition: Cash shells, special purpose acquisition company (SPACs), non-corporate, open-ended investment instruments such as open-ended investment companies and funds, unit trusts, ETFs and currency funds and split capital trusts.

subsectors.name: Life Insurance
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged principally in life and health insurance.

subsectors.name: Full Line Insurance
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide a wide range of insurance products such as a combination of life, property/casualty, and specialty insurance.

subsectors.name: Insurance Brokers
subsectors.definition: Insurance brokers and agents.

subsectors.name: Reinsurance
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged principally in reinsurance.

subsectors.name: Property and Casualty Insurance
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged principally in accident, fire, automotive, marine, malpractice and other classes of nonlife insurance.

sectors.name: Real Estate Investment and Services Development

subsectors.name: Real Estate Holding and Development
subsectors.definition: Companies that invest directly or indirectly in a variety of types of properties without a concentration on any single type.

subsectors.name: Real Estate Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide services to real estate companies but do not own the properties themselves. Includes agencies, brokers, leasing companies, management companies and advisory services.

subsectors.name: Diversified REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that invest in a combination of other defined REIT categories, none of which is the dominant business line.

subsectors.name: Health Care REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in healthcare facilities including hospitals, laboratories, nursing homes and assisted living properties.

subsectors.name: Hotel and Lodging REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in hotels, motels, resorts or other lodging properties.

subsectors.name: Industrial REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in industrial properties including industrial warehouses and distribution properties.

subsectors.name: Infrastructure REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in infrastructure assets including roads, bridges, tunnels, airports, power generation, fuels, pipelines, water and waste management and communication assets.

subsectors.name: Office REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in office properties.

subsectors.name: Residential REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in residential properties including manufactured homes, multifamily homes, apartments, and student housing properties.

subsectors.name: Retail REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in retail properties including malls, shopping centers, neighborhood and community shopping centers, strip malls, free standing stores, and factory outlets.

subsectors.name: Storage REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in public self-storage properties.

subsectors.name: Timber REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in timberland and timber-related products and activities.

subsectors.name: Other Specialty REITs
subsectors.definition: REITs that primarily invest in any single type of facility or property not specifically defined within another REITs industry.

subsectors.name: Auto Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide assistance to individual vehicle owners.

subsectors.name: Tires
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers, distributors and re-treaders of automobile, truck and motorcycle tires.

subsectors.name: Automobiles
subsectors.definition: Makers of passenger vehicles, including cars, sport utility vehicles (SUVs) and light trucks. Excludes makers of heavy trucks and makers of recreational vehicles (RVs and ATVs).

subsectors.name: Auto Parts
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of new and replacement parts for motorcycles and automobiles, such as engines, carburetors and batteries. Excludes producers of tires.

subsectors.name: Education Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that own and manage higher education systems, post-secondary degree programs or other educational services.

subsectors.name: Funeral Parlors and Cemetery
subsectors.definition: Companies that own and operate funeral homes, cemeteries, crematoriums and/or provide other funeral services.

subsectors.name: Printing and Copying Services
subsectors.definition: Companies specialising in printing, copying and/or similar solutions for individuals and small businesses.

subsectors.name: Rental and Leasing
subsectors.definition: Companies that lease automobiles, appliances, electronics or furniture to consumers.

subsectors.name: Storage Facilities
subsectors.definition: Companies that own and operate storage facilities (does not include companies structured as REITs).

subsectors.name: Vending and Catering Service
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide catering and food service and/or food ingredients to individuals or institutions.

subsectors.name: Consumer Services: Misc.
subsectors.definition: Consumer services companies that are not categorised in the other consumer services subsectors.

subsectors.name: Home Construction
subsectors.definition: Constructors of residential homes, including manufacturers of mobile and prefabricated homes intended for use in one place. Excludes companies earning material revenue from property rental or leasing.

subsectors.name: Household Furnishings
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of furniture, including chairs, tables, desks, and office furniture.

subsectors.name: Household Appliance
subsectors.definition: Companies that manufacture and market household electrical appliances.

subsectors.name: Household Equipment and Products
subsectors.definition: Companies that manufacture and supply various household products. Includes manufacturers of gardening tools, kitchen utensils, dishes and other home related products.

subsectors.name: Consumer Electronics
subsectors.definition: Companies involved in the application of technology and electronics to the consumer discretionary sector.

subsectors.name: Electronic Entertainment
subsectors.definition: Companies that design, manufacture and market video game software and related elements.

subsectors.name: Toys
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of toys, including playing cards, board games, stuffed animals and dolls.

subsectors.name: Recreational Products
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of recreational equipment not classified in other 'leisure goods' categories.

subsectors.name: Recreational Vehicles and Boats
subsectors.definition: Companies that design, manufacture and market recreation vehicles (RVs), motorcycles or passenger boats.

subsectors.name: Photography
subsectors.definition: Companies that produce and/or market professional and/or personal imaging products including digital cameras and film cameras.

subsectors.name: Clothing and Accessories
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of all types of clothing and accessories. Includes sportswear, sunglasses, eyeglass frames, leather clothing and goods.

subsectors.name: Footwear
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of shoes, boots, sandals, sneakers and other types of footwear.

subsectors.name: Luxury Items
subsectors.definition: Companies that manufacture and market valuable jewellery, watches and gemstones.

subsectors.name: Cosmetics
subsectors.definition: Companies that produce and market make-up and fragrance products (perfume). Excludes personal care products.

subsectors.name: Entertainment
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide various media services including feature films, music and television shows and stations.

subsectors.name: Media Agencies
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide a wide range of marketing and public relations services.

subsectors.name: Publishing
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide advertising and publishing services.

subsectors.name: Radio and TV Broadcasters
subsectors.definition: Companies operating commercial TV stations and/or radio broadcasting.

subsectors.name: Diversified Retailers
subsectors.definition: Retail outlets and wholesalers offering a wide variety of products including both hard goods and soft goods.

subsectors.name: Apparel Retailers
subsectors.definition: Retailers and wholesalers specialising mainly in clothing, shoes, sunglasses and other accessories.

subsectors.name: Home Improvement Retailers
subsectors.definition: Retailers and wholesalers concentrating on the sale of home improvement products.

subsectors.name: Specialty Retailers
subsectors.definition: Retailers and wholesalers concentrating on a single class of goods such as electronics, books, automotive parts.

subsectors.name: Airlines
subsectors.definition: Companies providing primarily passenger air transport. Excludes airports.

subsectors.name: Travel and Tourism
subsectors.definition: Companies providing travel and tourism related services including travel agents, online travel reservation services.

subsectors.name: Casino and Gambling
subsectors.definition: Providers of gambling and casino facilities including online casinos and racetracks.

subsectors.name: Hotels and Motels
subsectors.definition: Operators and managers of hotels, motels, lodges, resorts, spas and campgrounds.

subsectors.name: Recreational Services
subsectors.definition: Providers of leisure facilities and services including fitness centers, amusement parks, concerts and sports/e-sports event promotion.

subsectors.name: Restaurants and Bars
subsectors.definition: Operators of restaurants, fast-food facilities, coffee shops and bars.

subsectors.name: Brewers
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and shippers of cider or malt products such as beer, ale and stout.

subsectors.name: Distillers and Vintners
subsectors.definition: Producers, distillers, vintners, blenders and shippers of wine and spirits.

subsectors.name: Soft Drinks
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers, bottlers and distributors of non-alcoholic beverages such as soda, fruit juices, tea.

subsectors.name: Farming, Fishing, Ranching and Plantations
subsectors.definition: Companies that grow crops or raise livestock, operate fisheries or own nontobacco plantations.

subsectors.name: Food Products
subsectors.definition: Food producers including meatpacking, snacks, fruits, vegetables, dairy products and frozen seafood.

subsectors.name: Fruit and Grain Processing
subsectors.definition: Companies involved in value-added processes of agricultural products.

subsectors.name: Sugar
subsectors.definition: Companies that grow, refine, process and distribute sugar.

subsectors.name: Tobacco
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of cigarettes, cigars and other tobacco products.

supersectors.name: Personal Care, Drug and Grocery Stores

sectors.name: Personal Care, Drug and Grocery Stores

subsectors.name: Food Retailers and Wholesalers
subsectors.definition: Supermarkets, food-oriented convenience stores and food distributors.

subsectors.name: Drug Retailers
subsectors.definition: Operators of pharmacies including wholesalers and distributors.

subsectors.name: Personal Products
subsectors.definition: Makers and distributors of toiletries, personal-care and hygiene products.

subsectors.name: Nondurable Household Products
subsectors.definition: Producers and distributors of pens, paper goods, batteries, light bulbs, tissues, toilet paper and cleaning products.

subsectors.name: Miscellaneous Consumer Staple Goods
subsectors.definition: Consumer staples companies not classified in other subsectors.

subsectors.name: Construction
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide construction and infrastructure development services to private and/or public-sector clients.

subsectors.name: Engineering and Contracting Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide capital project planning and solutions.

subsectors.name: Building, Roofing/Wallboard and Plumbing
subsectors.definition: Companies that design, manufacture, market and/or install non-climate control systems.

subsectors.name: Building: Climate Control
subsectors.definition: Companies that design, manufacture, market and/or install air conditioning, heating and/or refrigeration systems.

subsectors.name: Cement
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of cement and cement-derived products.

subsectors.name: Building Materials: Other
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide materials to building and construction industry excluding air-conditioning, cement, heating, plumbing, roofing and wall boards.

subsectors.name: Aerospace
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers, assemblers and distributors of aircraft and aircraft parts for commercial or private air transport.

subsectors.name: Defense
subsectors.definition: Producers of components and equipment for the defense industry.

subsectors.name: Electrical Components
subsectors.definition: Makers and distributors of basic electrical parts for finished products such as radios, televisions and other consumer electronics.

subsectors.name: Electronic Equipment: Control and Filter
subsectors.definition: Companies primarily involved in providing mechanical and electronic security and/or filtration systems.

subsectors.name: Electronic Equipment: Gauges and Meters
subsectors.definition: Companies that design, manufacture and market products used to measure electric, gas, water and other data.

subsectors.name: Electronic Equipment: Pollution Control
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in production of pollution control equipment and related services.

subsectors.name: Electronic Equipment: Other
subsectors.definition: Companies that specialise in development and production of electrical devices/components marketed to business clients.

subsectors.name: Diversified Industrials
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in two or more industrial business activities, none of which is the dominant business line.

subsectors.name: Paints and Coatings
subsectors.definition: Companies that manufacture and distribute paint, material coatings, and resins.

subsectors.name: Plastics
subsectors.definition: Companies that manufacture and market plastic products or chemicals used to make plastic.

subsectors.name: Glass
subsectors.definition: Companies that manufacture various structural glasses.

subsectors.name: Containers and Packaging
subsectors.definition: Producers of packaging products and packaging related materials including cartons, plastic bottles, glass bottles, etc.

subsectors.name: Machinery: Industrial
subsectors.definition: Companies that design, develop, manufacture, sell, and support general industrial machines and parts.

subsectors.name: Machinery: Agricultural
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of a range of farming equipment.

subsectors.name: Machinery: Construction and Handling
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers of large-size industrial equipment for construction and ports.

subsectors.name: Machinery: Engines
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and distributors of energy output devices and components including diesel and gas engines.

subsectors.name: Machinery: Tools
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and marketers of value-adding equipment for heavy industries.

subsectors.name: Machinery: Specialty
subsectors.definition: Designers and manufacturers of specific types or groups of industrial machines and parts.

subsectors.name: Industrial Suppliers
subsectors.definition: Distributors and wholesalers of diversified products and equipment for commercial and industrial sectors.

subsectors.name: Transaction Processing Services
subsectors.definition: Providers of computerised transaction processing services including global payment services.

subsectors.name: Professional Business Support Services
subsectors.definition: Companies providing outsourced business operation services, consulting, corporate taxes and business decision tools.

subsectors.name: Business Training and Employment Agencies
subsectors.definition: Providers of business/management training courses and employment services.

subsectors.name: Forms and Bulk Printing Services
subsectors.definition: Providers of printed business products including forms, checks, labels and IDs.

subsectors.name: Security Services
subsectors.definition: Companies that provide security services, install, service and monitor alarm and security systems.

subsectors.name: Trucking
subsectors.definition: Companies providing commercial trucking or mass public bus services.

subsectors.name: Commercial Vehicles and Parts
subsectors.definition: Companies designing, manufacturing, and distributing light, medium and heavy-duty trucks and vans.

subsectors.name: Railroads
subsectors.definition: Companies operating railway systems for transporting goods and mass public rail services.

subsectors.name: Railroad Equipment
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers and suppliers of railroad supplies.

subsectors.name: Marine Transportation
subsectors.definition: Providers of on-water transportation for commercial markets, such as container shipping. Includes shipbuilders.

subsectors.name: Delivery Services
subsectors.definition: Operators of mail and package delivery services for commercial and consumer use.

subsectors.name: Commercial Vehicle-Equipment Leasing
subsectors.definition: Companies that rent and lease equipment to commercial clients such as rails, tankers, freight cars, aviation assets.

subsectors.name: Transportation Services
subsectors.definition: Companies providing services to the industrial transportation sector including airport and vehicle maintenance.

subsectors.name: Diversified Materials
subsectors.definition: Companies involved in manufacturing a diversified range of materials.

subsectors.name: Forestry
subsectors.definition: Owners and operators of timber tracts, forest tree nurseries and sawmills.

subsectors.name: Paper
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers of various paper products including office paper, cardboard and newsprint.

subsectors.name: Textile Products
subsectors.definition: Producers of various textile products such as cotton yarns, denims, and other fabrics.

subsectors.name: General Mining
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in the exploration, extraction or refining of minerals not defined elsewhere.

subsectors.name: Iron and Steel
subsectors.definition: Companies mining iron ore and producing steel products.

subsectors.name: Metal Fabricating
subsectors.definition: Manufacturers of fabricated metal components such as rings, hinges, springs.

subsectors.name: Aluminum
subsectors.definition: Companies mining or processing bauxite or producing aluminum bars and rods.

subsectors.name: Copper
subsectors.definition: Companies mining, extracting and distributing copper and related minerals.

subsectors.name: Nonferrous Metals
subsectors.definition: Producers and traders of metals and primary metal products other than iron, aluminum and steel.

subsectors.name: Diamonds and Gemstones
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in exploration for and production of diamonds and other gemstones.

subsectors.name: Gold Mining
subsectors.definition: Prospectors for and extractors or refiners of gold-bearing ores.

subsectors.name: Platinum and Precious Metals
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in exploration and production of platinum, silver and other precious metals.

subsectors.name: Chemicals: Diversified
subsectors.definition: Companies manufacturing a diversified range of industrial chemicals.

subsectors.name: Chemicals and Synthetic Fibers
subsectors.definition: Companies manufacturing and distributing chemical fibers.

subsectors.name: Fertilisers
subsectors.definition: Companies manufacturing and marketing nitrogen fertilisers and other agricultural chemicals.

subsectors.name: Specialty Chemicals
subsectors.definition: Companies manufacturing and marketing specific types or groups of chemicals.

subsectors.name: Integrated Oil and Gas
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in petroleum extraction, transportation, refining and marketing.

subsectors.name: Oil: Crude Producers
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in exploration for and drilling, production, and supply of crude oil or natural gas on land.

subsectors.name: Offshore Drilling and Other Services
subsectors.definition: Companies engaged in exploration for and drilling, production, and supply of crude oil or natural gas offshore.

subsectors.name: Oil Refining and Marketing
subsectors.definition: Companies primarily engaged in the refining and marketing of petroleum products.

subsectors.name: Oil Equipment and Services
subsectors.definition: Suppliers of equipment and services to oil or gas fields and offshore platforms.

subsectors.name: Pipelines
subsectors.definition: Operators of pipelines carrying oil, gas or other forms of fuel.

subsectors.name: Coal
subsectors.definition: Companies that mine, process and market coal.

subsectors.name: Alternative Fuels
subsectors.definition: Companies producing alternative fuels such as ethanol, methanol, hydrogen and bio-fuels.

subsectors.name: Renewable Energy Equipment
subsectors.definition: Companies developing or manufacturing renewable energy equipment using solar, wind, tidal, geothermal, hydro and wave sources.

subsectors.name: Alternative Electricity
subsectors.definition: Companies generating and distributing electricity from renewable sources such as solar, water, wind and geothermal.

subsectors.name: Conventional Electricity
subsectors.definition: Companies generating and distributing electricity through fossil fuels and nuclear energy.

subsectors.name: Multi-Utilities
subsectors.definition: Companies that engage in multiple utilities none of which individually generates 50% or more of revenue.

subsectors.name: Gas Distribution
subsectors.definition: Distributors of gas to end users.

subsectors.name: Water
subsectors.definition: Companies providing water to end users, including water treatment plants.

subsectors.name: Waste and Disposal Services
subsectors.definition: Providers of pollution control and environmental services for the management, recovery and disposal of solid and hazardous waste materials.
